purpose: Evaluate the frozen Train-696 EnOpt-style XGBoost models as supplementary comparators only after the primary fresh-validation gate is finalized.

evaluation.prediction: load each frozen XGBoost JSON model and call predict_proba; fitting, calibration, feature selection, and early stopping are prohibited
evaluation.normalization: for each primary, sensitivity, or individual-seed matrix, apply its corresponding frozen Train-696 per-receptor min-max bounds without clipping
evaluation.comparisons: report XGBoost-minus-QUBO and QUBO-minus-XGBoost BEDROC deltas for primary, sensitivity, mean-seed, and worst-seed summaries

prohibited_actions: run this evaluator before the primary fresh-validation result is finalized
refit either XGBoost model on validation data
change any XGBoost parameter or score transformation
change the primary QUBO pass/fail result
read or release the locked test split
interpretation_boundary: This analysis is a preregistered supplementary classical comparison. It cannot alter the primary QUBO gate, unlock test data, establish affinity or biological activity, or demonstrate quantum advantage. A tree model can exploit nonlinear score interactions without selecting a physically interpretable optimal receptor subset.
